title: Vada Pav
tagline: Mumbai's finest — spiced potato fritter in a soft bun.
macroNotes: Heads up — this is a cheat-day item. At ~990 kcal a serving it eats most of a 1600–1800 kcal day, and the protein-to-calorie ratio is poor because of the deep fry.
One vada pav instead of two → ~495 kcal, ~11 g protein.
Air-fry the vadas (200°C / 400°F, 12–15 min, spray with oil) instead of deep frying → saves ~250 kcal per serving.
Protein boost: stir 40 g of soaked, squeezed, finely chopped soya chunks into the potato masala. Adds ~10 g protein per serving for ~35 kcal each. Barely changes the taste.
Whole-wheat pav saves nothing calorically but adds ~4 g fibre.

items: 3 medium potatoes (~400 g / 2 cups mashed), boiled
1 pinch eno (fruit salt) — add right before frying
Optional: green chutney, dry garlic chutney, fried green chillies

steps.title: Boil the potatoes
steps.body: Boil 3 medium potatoes (~400 g) until fork-tender, peel, and mash them. Set aside.

steps.title: Make the paste
steps.body: In a mixer/grinder, blitz 8 cloves garlic + 3 green chillies + 1 tsp cumin seeds into a coarse paste. Don't make it a fine purée — a bit of texture is good.

steps.title: Temper it
steps.body: Heat 2 tbsp oil in a pan over medium heat. Add the garlic-chilli-cumin paste and fry gently until the raw smell goes, about 1–2 minutes.

steps.title: Season
steps.body: Add 1/2 tsp turmeric and 3/4 tsp salt. Stir for 30 seconds.

steps.title: Add the potato
steps.body: Tip in the mashed potato (~2 cups) and mix well, mashing everything together in the pan so the masala coats it evenly. Cook 2 minutes.

steps.title: Finish the masala
steps.body: Turn off the heat and stir in 3 tbsp chopped fresh coriander. That's your vada masala. Let it cool enough to handle.

steps.title: Shape
steps.body: Roll the masala into 4 balls (roughly golf-ball sized, ~90 g each).

steps.title: Make the batter
steps.body: Whisk 1 cup besan + 1/4 tsp turmeric + 1/2 tsp salt + 1 pinch eno with ~1/2 cup water, added a little at a time. You want it thick — pakora-batter thick, so it clings to the ball and doesn't run off. Do not add too much water. It should coat the back of a spoon heavily.

steps.title: Fry
steps.body: Heat ~2 cups oil to 175°C / 350°F (a drop of batter should sizzle and rise). Dip each ball fully in the batter and lower it into the oil. Fry 3–4 minutes, turning, until golden and crisp. Drain on paper towels.

steps.title: Assemble
steps.body: Slice the pav, add chutney if using, drop in the hot vada, press, and eat immediately.

notes: On the chillies: the original note says "tikha jaise chahiye waise kam-zyada kar lena" — dial the heat up or down as you like. 3 is medium.
source: Original note in Hinglish, translated and measured out. Scale up freely — 5–6 large potatoes makes about 10 vadas.
